title: Multisubject, multimodal face processing
description: A multimodal neuroimaging dataset comprising structural MRI, functional MRI, MEG, EEG, and diffusion-weighted imaging from 16 subjects performing a face recognition task. This is a curated subset of the original 19-subject dataset (subjects 01, 06, and 19 from the original cohort were excluded due to data quality issues, particularly poor EEG data). The remaining 16 subjects were selected to provide high-quality multimodal data for integration studies. The dataset includes anatomical images with defacing applied, blood oxygen level-dependent functional scans, magnetoencephalography recordings with digitized fiducials for co-registration, electroencephalography data, and diffusion-weighted imaging. This resource is designed to facilitate multimodal neuroimaging analysis and integration methods development.
methods_description: Data collection involved multiple imaging modalities: structural T1-weighted MPRAGE sequences (with defacing applied), task-based fMRI during face processing, MEG recordings with three anatomical fiducials (nasion and bilateral ears) digitized for MRI-MEG alignment, and EEG recordings. The face recognition task included presentations of famous faces, unfamiliar faces, and scrambled faces in initial and repeated conditions with varying delays.
